modules.summary: Provides the authentication interface for users to sign into Mifos with tenant, username, and password, handling validation and error feedback.
modules.where_it_fits: This is the application's entry point before accessing the top navigation (Institution, Accounting, Reports, Admin, etc.) and the left sidebar shortcuts; successful authentication redirects users into the Dashboard and the main application areas described in the navigation overview.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available; network connectivity to the authentication backend.

modules.summary: The Home Page presents a welcome card, recent activity search and quick access to the Dashboard, along with system version information.
modules.where_it_fits: Displayed immediately after successful login as the application's landing view, accessible via the top navigation bar context (e.g., after selecting Institution or from the persistent top bar) and serving as the launch point to the Dashboard and other sections reachable through the left sidebar and top navigation menus.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user session (successful login) with application context loaded

modules.summary: Overview page presenting client growth trends and high-level financial summary cards for a selected office.
modules.where_it_fits: This is an overview module accessed from the Home Page via the Dashboard button and appears in the main content area beneath the top navigation and breadcrumbs; it provides front‑line metrics (charts and summary cards) used before drilling into Clients, Accounts, or Reports.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available (selected or default)

modules.summary: Provides a global, case-insensitive search across Clients, Groups, Loans, and Savings accounts with grouped, partial-match results that link to entity detail pages.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar's search icon and available from any page after login, it serves as a session-wide navigation and discovery tool alongside the left sidebar shortcuts (Clients, Groups, Centers, Products) and breadcrumb navigation. It operates across Institution-related modules to quickly surface entities and jump to their detail pages.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.

modules.summary: Displays and manages the application’s clients via a searchable/filterable list, multi-step create wizard, bulk import, and detailed client lifecycle and account actions.
modules.where_it_fits: Located under Institution > Clients in the top navigation and reachable via the Clients icon in the left sidebar, this module is the primary client administration area within the application where users view client listings, import/create clients, and manage client status and related accounts as part of routine operations.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permission to access the Institution > Clients section (or Clients sidebar shortcut).

modules.summary: Provides listing, creation, bulk import, and detailed management of client groups including membership, group loans/savings, meetings, and group-level actions.
modules.where_it_fits: Located under Institution → Groups and reachable via the left-sidebar Groups icon, this module sits within the Client/Group Management area of the application and supports group lifecycle and operations (onboarding, meetings, loans/savings linkage, and administration).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permissions to view/manage groups and an active Institution/Office context; relevant client records exist for association when creating groups.

modules.summary: Management interface for creating, importing, viewing, and administering Centers—organizational units that contain Groups—with detail pages, action buttons, membership tabs, and collection-sheet generation.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Institution menu (Institution > Centers) and via the left sidebar Centers icon, this module lives among core client-organization management screens and is used after authentication when managing field-office structure and group aggregates; it links to Group detail pages and to account-related tabs (Loan/Savings) within the center lifecycle. It integrates with list and detail patterns used across the app (Material tables for listings, card layouts for details, and form-based create/import workflows).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with access to Institution features and at least one Office exists (or selected) to associate with a Center; relevant Groups may already exist for addition.

modules.summary: Lists existing loan products and provides a 6-step wizard for creating or editing detailed loan product configurations including terms, currency, charges, and accounting mappings.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the application's left sidebar Products shortcut and via the Institution area in the top navigation, the Loan Products page appears as an entity listing (with breadcrumbs) where administrators define product templates before loan accounts are created and managed elsewhere in the lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permission to view and manage product configuration (access to Products/Institution sections) and at least one Fund and any referenced Charges/GL accounts preconfigured if accounting mappings will be used.

modules.summary: Manages creation, configuration, and listing of savings, fixed deposit, and recurring deposit product definitions via a multi-step wizard and searchable product table.
modules.where_it_fits: Located under the Products section (accessible from the left sidebar shortcuts and within the Institution/Product area), this module is part of product lifecycle configuration used by administrators to define interest, fees, accounting mappings, and operational rules before deposit accounts are opened from the main account workflows. Breadcrumbs and Material Design tables situate it among other product and accounting configuration pages.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with product-management permissions and has navigated to the Products area (Savings Products page) in the application; no specific client or account is required.

modules.summary: Manages the catalog of share product definitions, including listing existing share products and a 7-step wizard for creating or editing product configurations and accounting mappings.
modules.where_it_fits: Located under the Products area accessible from the left sidebar (and reachable from the Institution/Product navigation), this module is an entity listing and detail workflow used during product setup and maintenance prior to creating share accounts or issuing shares. It sits alongside other product modules (Loan/Savings/Share Products) and ties into accounting and charges configuration in the application lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with product-management privileges and access to the Products section; the application context is on the Share Products listing page.

modules.summary: Manages fee and penalty definitions (charges) including creation, viewing, editing, and deletion of charge rules with attributes like applies-to, timing, calculation, amount, tax group, and payment mode.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is part of the product/financial configuration area of the application and is reachable from the left sidebar Products shortcut and related configuration sections in the top navigation (e.g., Institution/Accounting or Configuration areas); it is used when defining fees and penalties that apply to loans, savings, shares, and clients across the platform.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with an active organization selected and has permissions to manage product/charge definitions (admin/configuration role).

modules.summary: Defines and maintains floating interest rate schedules and their histories that automatically adjust linked loan product interest rates.
modules.where_it_fits: Located within the Products area reachable from the left sidebar shortcuts or via the Institution/Product navigation paths, this module is an entity listing/detail page in the main content area used during loan product configuration and rate management; it integrates with Loan Products so rate periods affect loan interest calculations. Breadcrumbs and Material table/listing conventions are used to navigate to and within this module.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings and the application is at the Products/Floatings Rates listing view

modules.summary: Manages definitions of delinquency ranges and buckets that classify overdue loans for reporting, provisioning, and collection workflows.
modules.where_it_fits: Located in the administrative/configuration area tied to product settings and portfolio reporting—reachable via the application's navigation where Products appear in the left sidebar and administrative pages are accessible from the top Institution menu (e.g., Institution → Admin or Reports); Delinquency buckets are associated with Loan Products and used during loan lifecycle processing.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with configuration/admin privileges and access to Loan Products (at least one loan product exists to which buckets can be linked).

modules.summary: Manages creation, approval, disbursement, lifecycle actions, and detailed views of individual loan accounts including repayment schedules, transactions, charges, collateral, and documents.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Institution > Clients flow and via the Clients shortcut in the left sidebar, the Loan Account module is reached when initiating a new loan from a Client Detail page or when opening an existing loan from client listings; it sits in the transaction lifecycle between loan product configuration (Loan Products) and accounting/repayment management (Transactions, Accounting).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with loan-management permissions and is either viewing a specific Client's Detail page (to start a new application) or selecting an existing Loan Account for inspection or action.

modules.summary: Manages creation, lifecycle and transaction handling for client savings accounts, including balances, status transitions, charges, and interest operations.
modules.where_it_fits: Reached from a Client's Detail page (accessible via Institution → Clients in the top nav or the Clients shortcut in the left sidebar) and displayed in the main content area beneath breadcrumbs; it links account operations (deposits, withdrawals, interest posting, status changes) into the broader accounting and reporting areas of the application.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user with permissions to manage accounts, viewing a specific Client Detail page, and at least one Savings Product configured/available for selection.

modules.summary: Manages creation, lifecycle transitions, and detailed viewing of client share investment accounts including applications, approvals, activations, purchases, redemptions, dividends, and associated charges.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Client Detail page under Institution → Clients (also reachable via the Clients icon in the left sidebar), the Share Account module appears as a detail and form workflow in the client management area and integrates into the account lifecycle and accounting/reporting flows shown in the breadcrumbs and main content area.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with permission to manage client accounts and is viewing a specific Client Detail page; at least one Share Product exists and the client may have active savings accounts for fee/deposit linkage.

modules.summary: Manages creation, lifecycle actions, and detailed views for Fixed Deposit (FD) and Recurring Deposit (RD) accounts including schedules, balances, interest calculations, and maturity operations.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessed from a Client Detail page within the Institution → Clients flow (and reachable via product/account shortcuts in the left sidebar), this module is part of the Products/Accounts area showing FD and RD accounts for individual clients. It integrates with product configuration (deposit products and interest rate charts) and appears alongside other account detail pages with tabs for Summary, Transactions, and Charges.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: An active client is selected on the Client Detail page and relevant FD/RD products and interest rate charts are configured in the system.

modules.summary: Displays a hierarchical tree of all General Ledger accounts by type with attributes, creation and detail/edit capabilities for GL accounts.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is part of the top-level Accounting area (accessible via the top navigation bar: Accounting → Chart of Accounts) and is used during chart setup and maintenance before or alongside journal entry and accounting operations. It links into the broader accounting workflow that includes Journal Entries & Closures and Accounting Rules.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with a role that has Accounting access and an organization instance configured; Accounting module accessible.

modules.summary: Manages general ledger journal entries and period closures by displaying, creating, and validating journal entries and recording accounting period closings.
modules.where_it_fits: Located under the top navigation Accounting menu and reachable via the left sidebar Accounting shortcuts, this module is part of the core Accounting section that operates on ledger data; it is used during regular accounting workflows to record transactions and to finalize periods by creating closures that lock prior dates against further postings.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with Accounting privileges, at least one Office and relevant GL accounts configured, and access to the Accounting section of the application.

modules.summary: Manages accounting rules that define debit/credit GL account behavior and maps system financial activities to specific General Ledger (GL) accounts for automated posting.
modules.where_it_fits: Located within the application's Accounting area (accessible from the top navigation bar under Accounting or via Institution → Accounting), this module sits on the configuration side of the accounting lifecycle to control how transactions and system-level financial events are posted to the ledger. It is used alongside chart-of-accounts and journal entry features to ensure automated and consistent accounting postings.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with accounting/configuration privileges and the organization's chart of GL accounts and Offices are already configured.

modules.summary: Manages provisioning criteria for categorizing overdue loans and generates provisioning journal entries that record calculated provisions per loan product and category.
modules.where_it_fits: Located within the Accounting area of the application (accessible from the top navigation Accounting menu and relevant accounting pages in the left sidebar), this module provides both the configuration of provisioning rules and the operational generation/review of provisioning journal entries as part of month-end or provisioning workflows tied to the loan portfolio lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with accounting/administrative privileges, a populated Chart of Accounts (GL accounts) exists, and the loan portfolio (including overdue loans) is present in the system.

modules.summary: Manages and displays the hierarchical branch/office structure, including listing, creation, and detail/edit of individual offices and their metadata.
modules.where_it_fits: Presented in the main content area and reachable from the top Institution menu (Institution > Offices) and the left sidebar organizational shortcuts; it sits early in organizational configuration and underpins client, staff, and accounting entities throughout the application lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permissions to view/manage offices and an existing Head Office configured as the root of the hierarchy.

modules.summary: Management interface for creating, listing, and editing employee (staff) records including office, loan officer assignment, contact details, and active status.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the application's navigation (via the top bar under Institution -> Admin and the left sidebar Employees shortcut), this module is part of institution/staff administration and supports operational workflows by supplying staff and loan officer assignments used across clients, groups, centers, and loan creation.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with staff-management permissions and at least one Office exists in the system.

modules.summary: Manages tellers and their cashier assignments, including creation, allocation, cash operations (allocate/settle), and transaction history in a tabular/detail view.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is part of the application's operational/administration functionality and is surfaced as an entity listing and detail area in the main content region (accessible from the top navigation's Institution area and other admin sections), using the same breadcrumbed layout and Material Design tables/forms as other entity pages.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with teller/cashier management permissions, and at least one Office and at least one Staff record exist in the system.

modules.summary: Management interface for creating and administering application users and role-based permissions, including user accounts, password policies, and a permissions matrix for roles.
modules.where_it_fits: Located under the top navigation Admin section (accessible from the Institution/Admin area) and surfaced via breadcrumbs in the main content area, this module is an administrative configuration area used to define who can access which menus, pages, and actions across the application lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with administrative privileges (permission to manage users/roles); Offices and Staff records exist if creating or linking users.

modules.summary: Displays a catalog of grouped financial and operational reports and allows generation of report outputs with parameterized filters and export options.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the application's top navigation under Reports (and via Institution → Reports), this module sits in the reporting/analytics area of the platform and is used after transactional data has been recorded to generate tables, charts, and exportable files for monitoring and analysis.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with permission to view reports and the system contains relevant transactional and master data (offices, products, loan/savings accounts, funds) for report parameters.

modules.summary: Handles one-time account-to-account transfers and management of recurring standing instructions that move funds between client accounts.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Institution menu and via account-related shortcuts in the left sidebar, this module appears in the core transaction flow where users view account details (breadcrumbs reflect the navigation path) and need to move funds or configure automatic transfers; it complements account and client management pages by executing debits/credits and scheduling recurring transfers.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permissions for account transfers and standing instruction management, and existing source and destination accounts (with sufficient available balance for a one-time transfer).

modules.summary: Configuration and management of tax components and tax groups that map tax rates to GL accounts and that can be applied to products and charge definitions to automatically calculate and post tax journal entries.
modules.where_it_fits: Located within the platform's administrative/accounting configuration area (accessible from the top navigation under Accounting or Admin and related from Products via the left sidebar), this module is part of the product and accounting setup lifecycle and is referenced when creating or editing savings products and charge definitions. It appears as entity listing and detail pages (breadcrumbs shown in the content area) consistent with the app's Material Design tables and forms.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with Accounting/Admin privileges and an existing Chart of Accounts (GL accounts) configured; relevant product or charge definitions exist when intending to reference a Tax Group.

modules.summary: A centralized administration module for configuring organization-wide settings such as holidays, working days, currencies, funds, payment types, and bulk data import.
modules.where_it_fits: Located under the top navigation Admin menu as the Organization section (visible via Admin > Organization Settings and reflected in breadcrumbs in the content area); it is part of the application's administrative configuration workflow used during initial setup and ongoing institutional maintenance.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with administrative privileges (access to the Admin menu) and an existing organization context configured in the application.

modules.summary: System Administration provides centralized management of scheduled jobs, global feature flags, code lists, custom data tables, and audit trails for the application.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is an administrative area exposed via the top navigation (Admin) and appears in the content area with breadcrumbs and icon-based sidebar context; it is used for system-wide configuration and background job lifecycle management across the application. It sits at the platform-level configuration layer, affecting Clients, Accounts, Products and reporting behaviors referenced elsewhere in the app.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges (role permitting access to Admin/System Administration) and viewing the Admin section in the top navigation.

modules.summary: Ends the current authenticated user session by clearing authentication credentials and returning the application to the login screen.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the user profile dropdown in the top-right of the persistent top navigation bar (near global search, language selection, notifications), this function is part of session lifecycle management and is reachable from any page across the app via the profile icon in the top navigation. After invocation it redirects to the Login page and prevents access to authenticated routes until re-authentication.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is currently authenticated with an active session and a valid authentication token.
